title: Time and Free Will

relations.label: Chapter I: The Intensity of Psychic States
Chapter II: The Multiplicity of Conscious States — The Idea of Duration
Chapter III: The Organization of Conscious States — Free Will

memes.label: Action
memes.description: Bergson's conception of action — the free act is not an uncaused event but the expression of the whole deep self in its continuous duration; action reveals the qualitative character of freedom

memes.label: Aesthetic Feeling
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of aesthetic feeling as the paradigm case of qualitative psychic transformation — the aesthetic emotion does not increase in intensity but changes in kind, through progressive stages of elevation, demonstrating that psychic states are qualitative, not quantitative

memes.label: Analysis
memes.description: Bergson's term for the intellectual operation that decomposes a whole into parts external to one another — analysis is useful for practical life and science but systematically distorts conscious reality by substituting spatial symbols for the interpenetrating flux of duration

memes.label: Anger
memes.description: Bergson analyzes anger as a violent emotion to demonstrate that emotional intensity is not a measurable magnitude but a qualitative disturbance of the organism — the intensity of anger is the degree of organic involvement.

memes.label: Association
memes.description: Bergson's critique of associationism — the psychological doctrine that mental states are linked by external laws of contiguity and resemblance; Bergson argues that association applies only to the superficial self, while the deep self is characterized by the interpenetration of states

memes.label: Associationism (associationnisme)
memes.description: The psychological doctrine that mental states are built from discrete, atomistic elements connected by laws of association — a view Bergson rejects as incapable of explaining the interpenetrating, qualitative multiplicity of consciousness

memes.label: Attention
memes.description: Bergson analyzes attention as an intermediate psychic state whose apparent intensity is reducible to the muscular sensations that accompany it — attention is the "reflective idea of knowing" coordinated with a system of bodily contractions, not a purely intellectual faculty

memes.label: Alexander Bain
memes.description: Scottish philosopher and psychologist whose associationist theory of nervous energy, account of space perception, and analysis of the conflict of motives are critically examined by Bergson.

memes.label: Becoming
memes.description: Bergson's concept of becoming (devenir) as the fundamental character of reality — consciousness is not a thing that changes but change itself, a continuous process of qualitative transformation in which states are in a perpetual state of becoming, never fixed, never complete

memes.label: Body
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of the body as the point of contact between inner duration and outer space — the body is not a separate substance from the mind but the surface on which qualitative conscious states register in spatial form

memes.label: Causality
memes.description: Bergson's critique of causality as a spatial concept illegitimately applied to conscious life — causal connection presupposes the mutual externality of terms, which belongs to space, not to the interpenetrating states of duration

memes.label: 'Chapter I: The Intensity of Psychic States'
memes.description: 'Chapter I: The Intensity of Psychic States'

memes.label: 'Chapter II: The Multiplicity of Conscious States — The Idea of Duration'
memes.description: 'Chapter II: The Multiplicity of Conscious States — The Idea of Duration'

memes.label: 'Chapter III: The Organization of Conscious States — Free Will'
memes.description: 'Chapter III: The Organization of Conscious States — Free Will'

memes.label: Change
memes.description: Bergson's distinction between qualitative change in duration (transformation of the whole state) and quantitative change in space (difference of degree along a scale) — the failure to distinguish these being the root of the confusion between quality and quantity

memes.label: Choice
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of choice as an expression of the whole self in duration — choice is not selection among externally arrayed possibilities but the organic unfolding of a unified consciousness, undermining both determinist and libertarian accounts

memes.label: Consciousness (conscience)
memes.description: The locus of duration in Bergson's philosophy — consciousness as the qualitative field where states interpenetrate rather than succeed one another discretely

memes.label: Charles Darwin
memes.description: British naturalist whose detailed descriptions of the physiological expression of emotion in animals and humans provide Bergson with empirical data for his analysis of emotional intensity.

memes.label: Deliberation
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of deliberation as a temporal process in which the self changes throughout, undermining the deterministic model of decision-making as a mechanical weighing of options.

memes.label: René Descartes
memes.description: French philosopher whose view of matter as homogeneous extensity and conservation of motion forms a key reference point for Bergson's critique of mechanistic determinism.

memes.label: Desire
memes.description: Bergson analyzes desire as a qualitative psychic state whose apparent increase in intensity is not a quantitative magnification but a progressive qualitative transformation involving the entire organism — desire, like emotion, develops by changing in kind, not merely in degree

memes.label: Determinism
memes.description: Bergson's critique of determinism as a spatializing error that treats conscious states as mutually external items subject to causal law — a confusion that dissolves when we grasp real duration

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: Bergson uses the dream state to illustrate the qualitative nature of duration — when the ego's contact with external objects is relaxed, time returns from quantity to quality, revealing the interpenetration of conscious states.

memes.label: Duration (durée)
memes.description: Bergson's central concept of duration as qualitative, heterogeneous, and continuous succession, distinct from mathematical time

memes.label: Dynamic
memes.description: Bergson's term for the qualitative, temporal mode of reality — the dynamic is the ongoing process of becoming that characterizes conscious life, as opposed to the static, spatial, mechanical representations that philosophy and science impose upon it

memes.label: Dynamism (dynamisme)
memes.description: The metaphysical view that reality is fundamentally active, driven by force or will rather than by the rearrangement of inert particles — Bergson's preferred alternative to mechanism, grounded in the experience of voluntary action and inner duration

memes.label: Effort
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of effort — particularly muscular effort — as a qualitative feeling of transition between inner states and outer action, revealing the continuity that quantitative psychology breaks apart

memes.label: Ego
memes.description: Bergson distinguishes between the superficial ego (spatialized, social, linguistic) and the deep ego (durational, qualitative, free) — the deep ego is the self that lives in real duration, whose states interpenetrate rather than being juxtaposed

memes.label: Emotion
memes.description: Bergson analyzes emotion as a qualitative state whose apparent intensity is not a measurable magnitude but a progressive alteration of the whole psychic field — each degree of emotion is a different emotion, not more of the same one

memes.label: Endosmosis
memes.description: Bergson's borrowed physical metaphor for the illicit mixing of qualitatively distinct domains — succession with externality, intensity with extension, freedom with necessity — that produces the hybrid, spatialized concepts which obstruct the direct intuition of duration and free will

memes.label: Evolution
memes.description: Bergson's use of evolution as a metaphor for the continuous, organic unfolding of the self in duration — a non-mechanical, non-teleological process of qualitative change.

memes.label: Extension
memes.description: Bergson's concept of extension as the defining property of material objects in space — the mutual externality of parts that makes measurement possible — and its illegitimate extension to conscious states

memes.label: Extensity (extensité)
memes.description: Bergson's technical term for the property of occupying space — the divisible, measurable, homogeneous spread that characterizes external objects and, by projection, spatialized conscious states

memes.label: Gustav Theodor Fechner
memes.description: Founder of psychophysics whose law relating stimulus intensity to sensation magnitude Bergson systematically critiques as the paradigmatic error of treating qualitative conscious states as quantitative magnitudes

memes.label: Feeling
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of feeling as a qualitative conscious state whose apparent intensity is resolved into qualitative multiplicity — feeling reveals the non-measurable nature of inner experience

memes.label: Free Will
memes.description: Bergson's conception of free will as the concrete expression of the whole qualitative self in a truly free act — distinct from both mechanistic determinism and abstract libertarian indeterminism

memes.label: Habit
memes.description: Bergson's concept of habit as an ingrained spatializing practice — the habitual projection of spatial distinctions onto temporal experience, reinforced by language and convention, that systematically obscures real duration

memes.label: Sir William Hamilton
memes.description: 苏格兰哲学家，柏格森在《时间与自由意志》中通过约翰·斯图亚特·密尔的批判间接引述，涉及自由意志与意识状态区分问题。

memes.label: Hermann von Helmholtz
memes.description: 德国生理学家与物理学家，柏格森在《时间与自由意志》中讨论其意志努力理论与颜色知觉的量化解释。

memes.label: Heterogeneity
memes.description: Bergson's concept of heterogeneity as the fundamental character of conscious life — qualitative diversity in which each element is unique and interpenetrates the whole, opposed to the homogeneity of space

memes.label: Homogeneity
memes.description: Bergson's concept of homogeneity as the defining property of space — the qualitative sameness of a medium in which parts are mutually external and interchangeable — and its illegitimate projection onto time and consciousness

memes.label: Idea
memes.description: Bergson's critique of the "idea" as a fixed, atomistic mental unit — ideas are not discrete building blocks of thought but artificial products of the spatializing intellect, extracted from the continuous flow of conscious life

memes.label: Illusion
memes.description: Bergson's diagnosis of the fundamental illusions produced by reflective consciousness — the systematic errors that arise when the intellect spatializes temporal experience.

memes.label: Imagination
memes.description: Bergson analyzes imagination as a mental faculty that inserts qualitative shades between distinct sensations, synthesizes temporal succession, and — when cut off from external constraint in dreams — reveals the spontaneous, non-spatial character of conscious life

memes.label: Impulse
memes.description: Bergson's concept of impulse as the inner qualitative tendency by which consciousness passes from one state to another — an organic movement of the whole self that is not reducible to mechanical causation or external determination

memes.label: Instinct
memes.description: Bergson's concept of instinct as a mode of knowledge and action that operates within duration — contrasted with the spatializing intellect, instinct grasps life from the inside and is closer to the reality of conscious experience than abstract reason

memes.label: Intelligence
memes.description: Bergson's concept of intelligence as the faculty of spatial analysis — a practical instrument for action on matter that is contrasted with instinct and intuition; intelligence grasps the discontinuous and static, and must be supplemented by intuition to apprehend duration and freedom

memes.label: Intensity (intensité)
memes.description: Bergson's concept of intensity as a qualitative, non-measurable property of conscious states, fundamentally distinct from extensive magnitude

memes.label: Interpenetration (interpénétration)
memes.description: Bergson's term for the way conscious states mutually pervade one another in duration — the defining characteristic of qualitative multiplicity, in contrast to the juxtaposition of objects in space

memes.label: Intuition
memes.description: Bergson's core philosophical method — the direct, sympathetic grasp of reality in its continuous becoming, as opposed to conceptual analysis which divides, immobilizes, and spatializes the flux of experience

memes.label: William James
memes.description: American psychologist and philosopher whose theory of the feeling of effort as centripetal (sensory) rather than centrifugal (motor) provides crucial empirical support for Bergson's critique of psychophysical measurement.

memes.label: Judgment
memes.description: Bergson's concept of judgment as a spatial and retrospective mental operation — judgment belongs to the domain of memory and reflection, not to immediate conscious experience, and is a poor guide to the nature of duration

memes.label: Immanuel Kant
memes.description: The philosopher whose distinction between matter and form of knowledge, theory of space as a priori intuition, and conception of freedom Bergson critically engages throughout Time and Free Will

memes.label: Language
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of language as a fundamentally spatializing force — language imposes fixed, discrete forms on the fluid, qualitative reality of conscious experience, making it a primary obstacle to grasping duration and free will

memes.label: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz
memes.description: German rationalist philosopher whose doctrine of preestablished harmony and conception of causality are examined by Bergson as the second pole of deterministic metaphysics, complementing Spinoza.

memes.label: Rudolf Hermann Lotze
memes.description: 德国哲学家与生理学家，提出"局部符号"（local signs）理论解释空间知觉的形成，柏格森将其与贝恩、冯特同列为后康德主义空间理论家。

memes.label: Magnitude
memes.description: Bergson's distinction between extensive magnitude (belonging to space, measurable) and intensive magnitude (a confused notion — intensity cannot be measured because it is purely qualitative)

memes.label: Matter
memes.description: Bergson's treatment of matter as the spatial residue of concrete experience — matter is what remains when qualitative differences are stripped away, yielding homogeneous extension amenable to mathematical physics

memes.label: Mechanical
memes.description: Bergson's critical term for the spatial, quantitative mode of explanation that reduces conscious processes to external relations between discrete elements — the mechanical is the primary obstacle to understanding duration, freedom, and the qualitative nature of psychic life

memes.label: Mechanism vs. Dynamism Debate
memes.description: The 19th-century philosophical debate between mechanism (explaining nature through mathematical laws) and dynamism (explaining nature through forces) — the intellectual context for Bergson's argument that both positions spatialize duration and miss the reality of free will

memes.label: Melody (mélodie)
memes.description: Bergson's central analogy for duration — a succession of notes that fuse into an indivisible, qualitative whole, illustrating how conscious states interpenetrate rather than stand side by side

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: Bergson's account of memory as the survival of the past in the present — not a storage of discrete representations but the continuous interpenetration of all lived experience in duration, making the self a qualitative accumulation rather than a series of states

memes.label: Metaphysics
memes.description: Bergson's critical treatment of metaphysics as a discipline that, when detached from the immediate data of consciousness, projects spatial and quantitative frameworks onto the qualitative reality of duration and freedom

memes.label: John Stuart Mill
memes.description: British empiricist philosopher whose associationist psychology and analysis of free will are examined by Bergson in the context of determinism.

memes.label: Mind
memes.description: Bergson's conception of mind as the faculty that spatializes experience — mind operates by breaking the continuous flow of duration into discrete, homogeneous units, enabling practical action but obscuring the real nature of inner life

memes.label: Moral Feeling
memes.description: Bergson analyzes moral feelings — particularly pity — as qualitative psychic states whose apparent intensity is a progressive transformation of the whole soul, not a measurable magnitude; the moral feeling changes in kind, not in degree, and is the foundation for his qualitative conception of free moral action

memes.label: Motion
memes.description: Bergson's distinction between physical motion in space (measurable, divisible) and the perception of motion in consciousness (qualitative, indivisible synthesis) — illustrating the broader quality–quantity distinction

memes.label: Multiplicity
memes.description: Bergson's distinction between two kinds of multiplicity — qualitative (interpenetrating states in duration) and quantitative (juxtaposed objects in space) — the key to his arguments about consciousness and free will

memes.label: Number
memes.description: Bergson's argument that the idea of number presupposes space — every number is a synthesis of units, and this synthesis requires the intuition of a homogeneous medium in which units can be juxtaposed

memes.label: Organic
memes.description: Bergson's concept of the organic as a mode of unity distinct from mechanical aggregation — organic wholes are characterized by the interpenetration of parts, making this concept central to his account of conscious life and his critique of mechanistic psychology

memes.label: Pain
memes.description: Bergson analyzes pain not as a measurable magnitude but as a qualitative state whose apparent intensity is actually the extent of the organism affected — pain as a case study in the reduction of quantitative intensity to qualitative extensity

memes.label: Parallelism (parallelisme)
memes.description: The doctrine that mental and physical states run in parallel without causal interaction — a view Bergson subjects to critique in Chapter III, arguing that it is an unstable compromise between dualism and materialism

memes.label: Perception
memes.description: Bergson's account of perception as a qualitative act of consciousness — not a passive registration of external stimuli but an active, synthetic process in which the whole self participates and which cannot be reduced to quantitative measurement

memes.label: Personality
memes.description: Bergson's concept of personality as the qualitative whole of the self expressed in each of its states — personality is not an aggregate of traits but a living unity whose entirety is present in every conscious act, and whose free acts are those that bear the mark of the whole personality

memes.label: Pleasure
memes.description: Bergson analyzes pleasure not as a measurable magnitude but as a qualitative signal — pleasure and pain are signs of the organism's future reaction, not quantities to be compared on a single scale, undermining the psychophysical project of measurement

memes.label: The Rise of Psychophysics
memes.description: The 19th-century scientific movement, led by Fechner and Weber, that attempted to establish mathematical laws relating physical stimuli to psychological sensations — the primary target of Bergson's critique of quantitative psychology in Time and Free Will

memes.label: Quality
memes.description: The fundamental character of conscious experience that resists quantification — Bergson argues that all psychic states are qualitative through and through, and that the confusion of quality with quantity is the source of the errors of determinism and psychophysics

memes.label: Quantity
memes.description: Bergson's critique of quantity as a category illegitimately applied to conscious states — the confusion of qualitative differences with quantitative magnitudes that underlies psychophysics and determinism

memes.label: Reality
memes.description: For Bergson, reality is not a static substance to be constructed by concepts but a continuous process of becoming given in immediate experience and grasped by intuition — the qualitative flux of consciousness, not the spatialized abstractions of the intellect

memes.label: Reason
memes.description: Bergson's critique of reason as a spatializing faculty — reason operates by breaking experience into discrete, mutually external terms and linking them by laws, which makes it suited to the physical world but systematically misleading when applied to consciousness and duration

memes.label: Reflection
memes.description: Bergson's concept of reflection as the strenuous philosophical effort to suspend spatial habits of thought and grasp duration directly — reflection is not abstract contemplation but a disciplined return to immediate conscious experience

memes.label: Rhythm
memes.description: Bergson's concept of rhythm as the qualitative organization of temporal experience — rhythm is not a quantitative measure of time but a qualitative patterning of duration that suspends ordinary perception and reveals the interpenetration of conscious states

memes.label: Théodule-Armand Ribot
memes.description: 法国心理学家，柏格森在《时间与自由意志》中引述其对注意力的运动表达理论，作为意识状态被空间化的关键证据。

memes.label: Self
memes.description: Bergson's distinction between the superficial self (spatialized, social, linguistic) and the deep self (durational, qualitative, free) — the free act is the expression of the whole deep self

memes.label: Sensation
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of sensation as a qualitative conscious state whose apparent intensity cannot be measured quantitatively — the entry point for his critique of psychophysics

memes.label: Simultaneity
memes.description: Bergson's concept of simultaneity as the connecting link between time and space — the means by which we spatialize duration by representing successive moments as coexisting points, enabling measurement but falsifying the nature of time

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: Bergson uses "soul" (âme) to designate the qualitative whole of conscious life — the interpenetrating totality of states in duration, contrasted with the spatialized ego of practical life and language

memes.label: Space
memes.description: In Bergson's philosophy, space is the homogeneous, quantitative medium of juxtaposition — the framework we project onto conscious states, thereby mistaking qualitative duration for measurable quantity

memes.label: Herbert Spencer
memes.description: British philosopher and evolutionist whose theory of gracefulness as economy of effort and physiological account of emotion are critically examined by Bergson.

memes.label: Baruch Spinoza
memes.description: Dutch rationalist philosopher whose metaphysics of substance and parallelism represents, for Bergson, the logical endpoint of the first conception of causality.

memes.label: Substance
memes.description: Bergson's critique of substance as a spatial category — the philosophical tendency to treat conscious states as if they inhered in an underlying substance, modeled on material objects in space, rather than as a continuous flow in duration

memes.label: Succession
memes.description: Bergson's distinction between two kinds of succession — qualitative succession in duration (without mutual externality) and spatial succession (with distinct, juxtaposed terms) — as the foundation of his critique of mechanistic causality

memes.label: Symbol
memes.description: Bergson's critique of symbolic representation — the tendency to substitute spatial symbols for temporal realities, which distorts our understanding of consciousness and duration.

memes.label: Synthesis
memes.description: Bergson's concept of mental synthesis as the act by which consciousness unifies a qualitative multiplicity — the synthesis of successive positions into a perception of motion, of units into a number, and of discrete sensations into a continuous whole — the fundamental operation of consciousness in duration

memes.label: Tendency
memes.description: Bergson's concept of tendency as the qualitative directionality of conscious life — a directional impetus that is not a measurable force, contrasted with mechanistically determined outcomes that spatialize inner states

memes.label: Tension
memes.description: Bergson's concept of tension as the qualitative internal state of consciousness that registers intensity, effort, and duration — a dynamic quality that is not a measurable quantity, used to describe both bodily sensation and the undivided activity of the free self

memes.label: Thought
memes.description: Bergson's critique of discursive thought as a spatializing faculty — shaped by practical needs and language, thought breaks the continuous flow of duration into discrete concepts, making it useful for action but distorting for metaphysics

memes.label: Time
memes.description: Bergson's critical distinction between homogeneous measurable time (a spatial construct) and real duration (la durée) — time as the intellect's spatial projection versus time as lived qualitative flow

memes.label: Voluntary
memes.description: Bergson's analysis of voluntary activity as the conscious, deliberate dimension of human action, serving as a foundation for understanding free will against determinism.

memes.label: Ernst Heinrich Weber
memes.description: German physiologist whose law of stimulus-perception relationship serves as the foundation of Fechner's psychophysics, critically examined by Bergson.

memes.label: Will
memes.description: Bergson's concept of will as the undivided expression of the whole self in duration — the will is not a separate faculty that chooses among motives but the organic act in which the self's entire history crystallizes into a free decision

memes.label: Wilhelm Wundt
memes.description: German physiologist and founder of experimental psychology, whose theory of space perception and analysis of sensation are discussed by Bergson.
